SEARCH
Module com.example.sol_eclipsado
Package com.example.sol_eclipsado.model

Class GameLogic

java.lang.Object
com.example.sol_eclipsado.model.GameLogic
public class GameLogic extends Object
Manages the core game state and logic for "El Sol Eclipsado." This class tracks the number of failed attempts, available aids, and determines the win/loss conditions based on the player's progress. Follows the Singleton pattern to maintain a single game state.

  • Method Details

    • getInstance

      public static GameLogic getInstance()
      Provides access to the single instance of the GameLogic.
      Returns:
      The current GameLogic instance.

    • getQuantityAid

      public int getQuantityAid()
      Returns:
      The number of remaining aids (hints).

    • getQuantityFailure

      public int getQuantityFailure()
      Returns:
      The number of failed attempts committed.

    • setQuantityAid

      public void setQuantityAid(int quantityAid)
      Parameters:
      quantityAid - Updates the number of available aids.

    • setQuantityFailure

      public void setQuantityFailure(int quantityFailure)
      Parameters:
      quantityFailure - Updates the failure count.

    • setWin

      public void setWin(boolean win)
      Parameters:
      win - Sets the current win status.

    • getWin

      public boolean getWin()
      Returns:
      true if the player has won the game.

    • hasLost

      public boolean hasLost()
      Checks if the player has lost based on the failure limit.
      Returns:
      true if failures are 5 or more, false otherwise.

    • checkWinCondition

      public boolean checkWinCondition()
      Verifies if all letters of the secret word have been found. In Java, char arrays are initialized with the null character '␀'.
      Returns:
      true if no empty spaces remain in the found letters array.

    • getRandomEmptyPosition

      public int getRandomEmptyPosition()
      Identifies all positions in the secret word that have not been revealed yet and selects one at random to assist the player.
      Returns:
      A random index of a hidden letter.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if no empty positions are left.
